summary.intent: Test PR #2587 (PyGithub 2.x upgrade + GitHub App authentication) by running minimal Jenkins jobs to verify all three auth code paths (M1: PyGithub REST client, M2: GIT_ASKPASS for git CLI, M3: URL normalization) work correctly without falling back to GITHUB_TOKEN.
summary.outcome: Successfully tested 3 Jenkins jobs (ocp4-scan-konflux passed, gen-assembly passed, build-fbc revealed 7 GITHUB_TOKEN fallbacks). Fixed build_repo.py by adding github_url parameter to _get_commit_hash, passing github_url in ensure_source, and using exectools.cmd_gather_async for local-only git commands. All 26 tests pass.

repo: PR #2587 introduces 3 auth mechanisms: M1 (get_github_client_for_org for PyGithub), M2 (get_github_git_auth_env for git CLI via GIT_ASKPASS), M3 (ensure_github_https_url for URL normalization)

GitHub App has 4 installations (operator-framework, openshift-priv, openshift, openshift-eng); get_github_git_auth_env() falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN when called without org/URL and multiple installations exist
GitHub App installation tokens use ghs_ prefix, distinguishing them from PATs
Jenkins jobs accept ART_TOOLS_COMMIT parameter (format: ghuser@commitish) to test PR branches

code.finding: _get_commit_hash static method didn't accept github_url parameter, causing GITHUB_TOKEN fallback when calling gather_git_async
ensure_source branch check (rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) wasn't passing github_url to gather_git_async
from_local_dir used gather_git_async for local-only git commands (git config --get, rev-parse), triggering unnecessary auth; should use exectools.cmd_gather_async directly

workflow: Minimal test coverage strategy: identify distinct code paths (M1, M2, M3) and find jobs exercising each rather than running all jobs
DRY_RUN=true is safer for testing than MOCK=true (which doesn't work for all jobs)
For local-only git commands that don't contact remotes, bypass gather_git_async (which injects auth) and use exectools.cmd_gather_async directly
Jenkins MCP server can programmatically trigger builds and fetch logs for verification

friction: Jenkins job paths use URL encoding (build%2Focp4-scan-konflux) which complicates MCP queries
ToolSearch for Jenkins MCP server tools didn't return expected results initially
Bash Read tool's startLine/endLine parameters didn't work as expected, returning wrong line ranges
GIT_PASSWORD tokens logged in plaintext (security concern, being addressed separately)
Required multiple iterations to trace root cause of GITHUB_TOKEN fallback through doozer code paths
open_items: Commit and push build_repo.py fixes to PR branch
Re-run build-fbc job after fix to confirm GITHUB_TOKEN is no longer used
Update GitHub PR comment with build-fbc test results after verification
GIT_PASSWORD plaintext logging being tracked in separate chat
